findings.title: Aborted network request to Google Analytics
findings.description: A POST request to Google Analytics was aborted, which may indicate a problem with tracking or a browser extension blocking the request.
findings.fix_prompt: Check the Google Analytics configuration and ensure that the tracking code is correctly set up. If not needed, consider removing the Google Analytics script.

findings.title: Outdated or placeholder content
findings.description: The page contains 'React' repeated multiple times, which seems unnecessary and could be optimized for better readability.
findings.fix_prompt: Review and refine the content to remove any redundant information and ensure clarity.

findings.title: Inconsistent spacing and alignment
findings.description: The layout seems mostly consistent, but a detailed review is needed to ensure proper spacing and alignment across different screen sizes.
findings.fix_prompt: Review the CSS styles to ensure consistent spacing and alignment across the page, especially on different devices.

raw_status.summary: The page appears to be mostly functional and visually consistent, but there are a few minor issues related to performance and content that should be addressed. Overall, the page scores 90 out of 100 in terms of quality.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://todomvc.com/examples/react/dist/ and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [LOW] Aborted network request to Google Analytics
   Fix: Check the Google Analytics configuration and ensure that the tracking code is correctly set up. If not needed, consider removing the Google Analytics script.

2. [LOW] Outdated or placeholder content
   Fix: Review and refine the content to remove any redundant information and ensure clarity.

3. [LOW] Inconsistent spacing and alignment
   Fix: Review the CSS styles to ensure consistent spacing and alignment across the page, especially on different devices.

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.

Journey context:

before_snapshot.title: TodoMVC: React
before_snapshot.text_snippet:
React
React
Source
TypeScript + React
Demo, Source

React is a JavaScript library for creating user interfaces. Its core principles are declarative code, efficiency, and flexibility. Simply specify what your component looks like and React will keep it up-to-date when the underlying data changes.

React
Official Resources
Quick Start
API Reference
Philosophy
React Community
Community
ReactJS on Stack Overflow
If you have other helpful links to share, or find any of the links above no longer work, please let us know.

findings.title: Console error
findings.description: The page has a console error: 'Failed to load resource: net::ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED'. This could indicate a problem with a third-party resource.
findings.fix_prompt: Check the Optimizely script tag and ensure the URL is correct or remove the tag if not needed.

findings.title: Failed network request
findings.description: There is a failed network request to Optimizely. This could be causing a minor performance issue.
findings.fix_prompt: Verify the Optimizely script is necessary and correctly configured.

findings.title: Missing alt text for image
findings.description: The 'Fork me on GitHub' image is missing alt text.
findings.fix_prompt: Add alt text to the image: .

raw_status.summary: The page has some issues with failed network requests and missing content, but overall looks professional. There are a few potential accessibility issues that should be addressed.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://www.advantageonlineshopping.com and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [HIGH] Failed network requests
   Fix: Check Google Analytics configuration and ensure proper tracking code implementation

2. [MEDIUM] Empty or missing page content
   Fix: Verify that the page content is properly loaded and rendered

3. [LOW] Missing alt text for images
   Fix: Add alt text to all images on the page

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.
